sections.label: Section 635
sections.translation: rose the storm by the wrath of the gods upon the naval host and passed away ?

sections.label: Section 636
sections.translation: An auspicious day one should not mar with a tale of misfortune — the honor due to the gods keeps them apart . [*] When a messenger with gloomy countenance reports to a people dire disaster of its army ’ s rout —

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: πόλει μὲν ἕλκος ἓν τὸ δήμιον τυχεῖν ,
sections.translation: one common wound inflicted on the State , while from many a home many a victim is devoted to death by the two-handled whip beloved of Ares , destruction double-armed , a gory pair — when , I say , he is packed with woes like this ,

sections.label: Section 645
sections.translation: he should sing the triumph-song of the Avenging Spirits . But when one comes with glad news of deliverance to a city rejoicing in its happiness — how shall I mix fair with foul in telling of the storm , not unprovoked by the gods ’ wrath , that broke upon the Achaeans ?

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: ξυνώμοσαν γάρ , ὄντες ἔχθιστοι τὸ πρίν ,
sections.translation: For fire and sea , beforehand bitterest of foes , swore alliance and as proof destroyed the unhappy Argive army . In the night-time arose the mischief from the cruel swells . Beneath blasts from Thrace ship dashed against ship ;

sections.label: Section 655
sections.translation: and they , gored violently by the furious hurricane and rush of pelting rain , were swept out of sight by the whirling gust of an evil shepherd . [*] But when the radiant light of the sun rose we beheld the Aegean flowering with corpses

sections.label: Section 660
sections.translation: of Achaean men and wreckage of ships . Ourselves , however , and our ship , its hull unshattered , some power , divine not human , preserved by stealth or intercession , laying hand upon its helm ; and Savior Fortune chose to sit aboard our craft

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: ὡς μήτ ἐν ὅρμῳ κύματος ζάλην ἔχειν
sections.translation: so that it should neither take in the swelling surf at anchorage nor drive upon a rock-bound coast . Then , having escaped death upon the deep , in the clear bright day , scarce crediting our fortune , we brooded in anxious thought over our late mischance ,

sections.label: Section 670
sections.translation: our fleet distressed and sorely buffeted . So now , if any of them still draw the breath of life , they speak of us as lost — and why should they not ? We think the same of them . But may all turn out for the best ! For Menelaus , indeed ;

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: πρῶτόν τε καὶ μάλιστα προσδόκα μολεῖν .
sections.translation: first and foremost expect him to return . At least if some beam of the sun finds him alive and well , by the design of Zeus , who has not yet decided utterly to destroy the race , there is some hope that he will come home again .

sections.label: Section 680
sections.translation: Hearing so much , be assured that you hear the truth . Exit

sections.label: Section 1.681
sections.translation: Who can have given a name so altogether true — was it some power invisible guiding his tongue aright by forecasting of destiny ?—

sections.label: Section 1.685
sections.translation: who named that bride of the spear and source of strife with the name of Helen ? For , true to her name , a Hell she proved to ships , Hell to men , Hell to city ,

sections.label: Section 1.690
sections.translation: when stepping forth from her delicate and costly-curtained bower , she sailed the sea before the breath of earth-born Zephyrus . And after her a goodly host of warrior

sections.label: Section 1.695
sections.translation: huntsmen followed on the oars ’ vanished track in pursuit of a quarry that had beached its boat on Simois ’ leafy banks — in a strife to end in blood .

sections.label: Section 1.699
sections.translation: To Ilium , its purpose fulfilling ,

sections.label: Section 1.700
sections.translation: Wrath brought a marriage rightly named a mourning , [*] exacting in later time requital for the dishonor done to hospitality and to Zeus , the partaker of the hearth ,

sections.label: Section 1.710
sections.translation: But Priam ’ s city has learned , in her old age , an altered strain , and now , I trust , wails a loud song , full of lamentation , calling Paris evil-wed ; for she has born the burden of a life in which everything was destroyed , a life full of lamentation because of

sections.label: Section 1.715
sections.translation: the wretched slaughter of her sons

sections.label: Section 1.716
sections.translation: Even so a man reared in his house a lion ’ s whelp , robbed of its mother ’ s milk yet still desiring the breast . Gentle it was

sections.label: Section 2.720
sections.translation: in the prelude of its life , kindly to children , and a delight to the old . Much did it get , held in arms like a nursling child , with its

sections.label: Section 2.725
sections.translation: bright eye turned toward his hand , and fawning under compulsion of its belly ’ s need .

sections.label: Section 2.727
sections.translation: But brought to full growth by time it showed the nature it had from its parents . Unbidden , as payment for its fostering ,

sections.label: Section 2.730
sections.translation: it prepared a feast with ruinous slaughter of the flocks ; so that the house was defiled with blood , and whose who lived there could not control their anguish , and great was the carnage far and wide .

sections.label: Section 2.735
sections.original: ἐκ θεοῦ δ ἱερεύς τις ἄ-
sections.translation: A priest of ruin , by order of a god , it was reared in the house .

sections.label: Section 3.737
sections.original: πάραυτα δ ἐλθεῖν ἐς Ἰλίου πόλιν
sections.translation: At first , I would say , there came to Ilium the spirit of unruffled calm ,

sections.label: Section 3.740
sections.translation: a delicate ornament of wealth , a darter of soft glances from the eye , love ’ s flower that stings the heart . Then , swerving from her course , she brought

sections.label: Section 3.745
sections.translation: her marriage to a bitter end , sped on to the children of Priam under escort of Zeus , the warder of host and guest , ruining her sojourn and her companions , a vengeful Fury who brought tears to brides .

sections.label: Section 3.750
sections.original: παλαίφατος δ ἐν βροτοῖς γέρων λόγος
sections.translation: A venerable utterance proclaimed of old has been fashioned among mankind : the prosperity of man , when it has come to full growth , engenders offspring and does not die childless ,

sections.label: Section 3.755
sections.translation:
and from his good fortune there springs up insatiable misery .
But I hold my own mind and think apart from other men . It is the evil deed that afterwards begets more iniquity

sections.label: Section 3.760
sections.translation: like its own breed ; but when a house is righteous , the lot of its children is blessed always .

sections.label: Section 4.763
sections.translation: But an old Hubris tends to bring forth

sections.label: Section 4.765
sections.translation: in evil men , sooner or later , at the fated hour of birth , a young Hubris and that irresistible , unconquerable , unholy spirit , Recklessness ,

sections.label: Section 4.770
sections.translation: and for the household black Curses , which resemble their parents .

sections.label: Section 4.772
sections.translation: But Righteousness shines in smoke-begrimed dwellings

sections.label: Section 4.775
sections.translation: and esteems the virtuous man . From gilded mansions , where men ’ s hands are foul , she departs with averted eyes and makes her way to pure homes ; she does not worship the power

sections.label: Section 4.780
sections.translation: of wealth stamped counterfeit by the praise of men , and she guides all things to their proper end .

sections.label: Section 782
sections.translation: Enter Agamemnon and Cassandra , in a chariot , with a numerous retinue All hail , my King , sacker of Troy , off-spring of Atreus !

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: πῶς σε προσείπω ; πῶς σε σεβίζω
sections.translation: How shall I greet you ? How shall I do you homage , not overshooting or running short of the due measure of courtesy ? Many of mortal men put appearance before truth and thereby transgress the right .

sections.label: Section 790
sections.translation: Every one is ready to heave a sigh over the unfortunate , but no sting of true sorrow reaches the heart ; and in seeming sympathy they join in others ’ joy , forcing their faces into smiles .

sections.label: Section 795
sections.translation:
But whoever is a discerning shepherd of his flock cannot be deceived by men ’ s eyes which , while they feign loyalty of heart , only fawn upon him with watery affection . [*]
Now in the past , when you marshaled the army in Helen ’ s cause ,

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: Ἑλένης ἕνεκ , οὐ γάρ σ [*] ἐπικεύσω ,
sections.translation: you were depicted in my eyes ( for I will not hide it from you ) most ungracefully and as not rightly guiding the helm of your mind in seeking through your sacrifices to bring courage to dying men .

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: νῦν δ οὐκ ἀπ ἄκρας φρενὸς οὐδ ἀφίλως
sections.translation: But now , from the depth of my heart and with no lack of love ⋯ their toil is joy to those who have won success . In course of time you shall learn by enquiry who of your people has been an honest , and who an unfitting guardian of the State .

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: πρῶτον μὲν Ἄργος καὶ θεοὺς ἐγχωρίους
sections.translation: Argos first , as is right and proper , I greet , and her local gods who have helped me to my safe return and to the justice I exacted from Priam ’ s town . For listening to no pleadings by word of mouth , [*] without dissenting voice , they cast into the

sections.label: Section 815
sections.translation: bloody urn their ballots for the murderous destroying of Ilium ; but to the urn of acquittal that no hand filled , Hope alone drew near . The smoke even now still declares the city ’ s fall . Destruction ’ s blasts still live , and

sections.label: Section 820
sections.translation: the embers , as they die , breathe forth rich fumes of wealth . For this success we should render to the gods a return in ever-mindful gratitude , seeing that we have thrown round the city the toils of vengeance , and in a woman ’ s cause it has been laid low by the fierce Argive beast ,

sections.label: Section 825
sections.translation:
brood of the horse , [*] a shield-armed folk , that launched its leap when the Pleiades waned . Vaulting over its towered walls , the ravening lion lapped up his fill of princely blood .
For the gods then I have stretched out this prelude .

sections.label: Section 830
sections.original: τὰ δ ἐς τὸ σὸν φρόνημα , μέμνημαι κλύων ,
sections.translation: But , touching your sentiments — which I heard and still bear in memory — I both agree and you have in me an advocate . For few there are among men in whom it is inborn to admire without envy a friend ’ s good fortune . For the venom of malevolence settles upon the heart and

sections.label: Section 835
sections.translation: doubles the burden of him who suffers from that plague : he is himself weighed down by his own calamity , and groans to see another ’ s prosperity . From knowledge — for well I know the mirror of companionship — I may call a shadow of a shade

sections.label: Section 840
sections.translation:
those who feigned exceeding loyalty to me . [*] Only Odysseus , the very man who sailed against his will , once harnessed , proved my zealous yoke-fellow . This I affirm of him whether he is alive or dead .
But , for the rest , in what concerns the State and public worship ,

sections.label: Section 845
sections.translation: we shall appoint open debates and consider . Where all goes well , we must take counsel so that it may long endure ; but whenever there is need of healing remedy , we will by kind appliance of cautery or the knife

sections.label: Section 850
sections.translation:
endeavor to avert the mischief of the disease .
And now I will pass to my palace halls and to my household hearth , and first of all pay greeting to the gods . They who sent me forth have brought me home again . May victory , now that it has attended me , remain ever with me constant to the end ! He descends from his chariot ; enter Clytaemestra , attended by maidservants carrying purple tapestries

sections.label: Section 855
sections.translation:
Citizens of Argos , you Elders present here , I shall not be ashamed to confess in your presence my fondness for my husband — with time diffidence dies away in humans .
Untaught by others , I can tell of my own weary life

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: τοσόνδ ὅσον περ οὗτος ἦν ὑπ Ἰλίῳ .
sections.translation: all the long while my husband was beneath Ilium ’ s walls . First and foremost , it is a terrible evil for a wife to sit forlorn at home , severed from her husband , always hearing many malignant rumors , and for one messenger after another

sections.label: Section 865
sections.translation: to come bearing tidings of disaster , each worse than the last , and cry them to the household . And as for wounds , had my husband received so many as rumor kept pouring into the house , no net would have been pierced so full of holes as he . Or if he had died as often as reports claimed ,

sections.label: Section 870
sections.translation: then truly he might have had three bodies , a second Geryon , [*] and have boasted of having taken on him a triple cloak of earth ample that above , of that below I speak not , one death for each different shape . Because of such malignant tales as these ,

sections.label: Section 875
sections.translation: many times others have had to loose the high-hung halter from my neck , held in its strong grip . It is for this reason , in fact , that our boy , Orestes , does not stand here beside me , as he should — he in whom rest the pledges of my love and yours . Nor should you think this strange .

sections.label: Section 880
sections.translation: For he is in the protecting care of our well-intentioned ally , Strophius of Phocis , who warned me of trouble on two scores — your own peril beneath Ilium ’ s walls , and then the chance that the people in clamorous revolt might overturn the Council , as it is natural

sections.label: Section 885
sections.translation: for men to trample all the more upon the fallen . Truly such an excuse supports no guile .

sections.label: Section 887
sections.translation: As for myself , the welling fountains of my tears are utterly dried up — not a drop remains . In night-long vigils my eyes are sore
